mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals a deteriorating labor market and reduced household income, which suppresses corporate earnings growth and drives negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations when UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer spending, which directly reduces near-term corporate revenue expectations and forces negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: Joint negative growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields positive 20-day forward returns in over 60 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production levels reflect robust manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, supporting immediate corporate earnings revisions and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between INDPRO and 5-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising inflation expectations force the central bank to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 220 during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid job growth combined with rising unemployment indicates labor market inefficiency and potential wage-price spiral risks, prompting market participants to price in future monetary tightening and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Simultaneous increases in PAYEMS and UNRATE yield positive 20-day returns in over 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in real GDP growth below 1.0 percent signals economic stagnation, reducing corporate profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of quarters following sub-1.0 percent GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained regime, rising industrial production alleviates bottlenecks and lowers input costs, improving corporate margins and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the INDPRO-CPIAUCSL interaction term remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window during supply bottlenecks.
